description: Wires @xterm/addon-search into each Terminal leaf and builds a TerminalSearch overlay that opens on Ctrl+F, supports search-as-you-type with a 150ms debounce, case-insensitive default with an Aa toggle, a match indicator, and Enter/F3 navigation. Search addon ref and isSearchOpen state live inside Terminal.tsx for automatic per-pane scoping in split views; the KeyboardShortcuts panel is updated in the same issue.

successCriteria.description: @xterm/addon-search@^0.16.0 added to apps/desktop/package.json dependencies and pnpm-lock.yaml updated via pnpm install.

Terminal.tsx imports SearchAddon from @xterm/addon-search, stores it in searchAddonRef (useRef<SearchAddon | null>(null)), instantiates it after new XTerm() and calls xterm.loadAddon(searchAddon) before xterm.open(container).

Terminal.tsx adds isSearchOpen boolean state and extends attachCustomKeyEventHandler to intercept Ctrl+F (setIsSearchOpen(true), return false) and — when isSearchOpen is true — F3/Shift+F3 for findNext/findPrevious, each returning false.

TerminalSearch.tsx renders as an absolute-positioned overlay (top-right, z-index 20) inside .terminal-container with: controlled text input, Previous/Next arrow buttons, match indicator label, Aa case-sensitive toggle, and X close button.

Search-as-you-type debounced at 150ms via useRef + setTimeout. Empty query produces no highlights. No-match state applies .terminal-search-input--no-match CSS modifier (red border). Closing calls xtermRef.current?.focus() to restore xterm focus.
Each Terminal leaf owns its own SearchAddon ref and isSearchOpen state. Split panes and multiple tabs have fully independent search state with no shared global.
KeyboardShortcuts.tsx Terminal SHORTCUT_GROUPS entry gains { keys: ['Ctrl', 'F'], description: 'Find in terminal buffer' } as the final entry in its shortcuts array.
apps/desktop/src/components/terminal/__tests__/terminalSearch.test.ts contains vitest tests for exported pure helpers formatMatchCount and isNoMatch; pnpm test passes with all new tests green.

phases.title: Phase 1 — Install @xterm/addon-search and wire SearchAddon into Terminal.tsx
phases.content:
This phase installs the search addon package and integrates it into Terminal.tsx with no visible UI — just the addon wired up, state scaffolded, and the Ctrl+F / F3 key intercepts added to the custom key handler. The goal is a build-verified foundation that Phase 2 layers the TerminalSearch component on top of.

Add '@xterm/addon-search': '^0.16.0' to the dependencies section of apps/desktop/package.json, below @xterm/addon-web-links to keep the xterm addon block together. Run pnpm install from the repo root to update pnpm-lock.yaml.

In Terminal.tsx: import SearchAddon from '@xterm/addon-search'. Declare const searchAddonRef = useRef<SearchAddon | null>(null) and const searchQueryRef = useRef<string>('') alongside fitAddonRef. Declare const [isSearchOpen, setIsSearchOpen] = useState(false) near the other useState calls. Inside the init useEffect, after instantiating FitAddon and WebLinksAddon, instantiate const searchAddon = new SearchAddon() and call xterm.loadAddon(searchAddon) before xterm.open(container). After open, set searchAddonRef.current = searchAddon. In the cleanup return, set searchAddonRef.current = null.

Extend attachCustomKeyEventHandler with two new intercepts before the final return true: (1) if e.type === 'keydown' && e.ctrlKey && !e.shiftKey && e.key === 'f', call setIsSearchOpen(true) and return false; (2) if e.type === 'keydown' && e.key === 'F3' && isSearchOpenRef.current is true, call searchAddonRef.current?.findNext(searchQueryRef.current, { caseSensitive: false }) on plain F3 or findPrevious on Shift+F3, and return false. Use const isSearchOpenRef = useRef(false) kept in sync via a useEffect — this avoids the stale-closure trap inside the key handler.
tasks: Add '@xterm/addon-search': '^0.16.0' to the dependencies section of apps/desktop/package.json below @xterm/addon-web-links
Run pnpm install from the repo root to resolve the new package and update pnpm-lock.yaml
In Terminal.tsx, add import { SearchAddon } from '@xterm/addon-search' to the addon imports block
Declare const searchAddonRef = useRef<SearchAddon | null>(null), const searchQueryRef = useRef<string>(''), and const isSearchOpenRef = useRef(false) alongside fitAddonRef
Declare const [isSearchOpen, setIsSearchOpen] = useState(false) and add a useEffect that syncs isSearchOpenRef.current = isSearchOpen on every change

In the init useEffect, instantiate SearchAddon, call xterm.loadAddon(searchAddon) before xterm.open(container), then set searchAddonRef.current = searchAddon; in cleanup set searchAddonRef.current = null
Extend attachCustomKeyEventHandler: intercept Ctrl+F (keydown, ctrlKey, !shiftKey, key === 'f') — call setIsSearchOpen(true), return false
Extend attachCustomKeyEventHandler: intercept F3 and Shift+F3 when isSearchOpenRef.current is true — call findNext/findPrevious with searchQueryRef.current and { caseSensitive: false }, return false
Run pnpm typecheck to verify zero TypeScript errors
Run pnpm build to verify tsc -b strict compile passes
verification: apps/desktop/package.json dependencies section contains '@xterm/addon-search' and pnpm-lock.yaml has been updated with the resolved package
pnpm typecheck passes with no errors introduced in Terminal.tsx
pnpm build passes (tsc -b strict) with no new type errors
searchAddonRef, searchQueryRef, isSearchOpenRef, and isSearchOpen state are all declared in Terminal.tsx; SearchAddon is imported from '@xterm/addon-search'

phases.title: Phase 2 — TerminalSearch component, CSS, Terminal.tsx render wiring, tests, and KeyboardShortcuts update
phases.content:
This phase delivers all visible user-facing work: the TerminalSearch overlay component, its CSS, the wiring into Terminal.tsx's JSX, vitest tests for pure helpers, and the KeyboardShortcuts panel entry.

Create apps/desktop/src/components/terminal/TerminalSearch.tsx. Export two pure helpers at the top of the file so the test file can import them: formatMatchCount(found: boolean, query: string): string returns '' when query is empty, 'No results' when query is non-empty and found is false, 'Match found' when found is true; isNoMatch(found: boolean, query: string): boolean returns true only when query.trim() is non-empty and found is false. Props interface: { searchAddon: SearchAddon; onClose: () => void; onQueryChange: (q: string) => void }. Internal state: query string, caseSensitive boolean (default false), found boolean (default true). Debounce timer via debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null). On query or caseSensitive change, clear any pending debounceRef and schedule a 150ms callback that calls searchAddon.findNext(query, { caseSensitive, incremental: false }) — store the boolean result in found state. When query is empty, call searchAddon.findNext('', {}) to clear highlights. Also call onQueryChange(query) inside the debounce callback so parent keeps searchQueryRef in sync. On mount, call inputRef.current?.focus() via a useEffect with empty deps. Key handler on the input: Escape calls onClose; Enter calls searchAddon.findNext(query, { caseSensitive }); Shift+Enter calls searchAddon.findPrevious(query, { caseSensitive }). Previous button calls findPrevious; Next button calls findNext. Aa button toggles caseSensitive. X button calls onClose. Apply className 'terminal-search-input--no-match' to the input when isNoMatch(found, query) is true.

IMPORTANT API constraint: SearchAddon.findNext returns only a boolean (found / not found); the addon does NOT expose a total match count via its public API. The match indicator therefore shows 'Match found' or 'No results' rather than 'N of M'. Document this in a brief code comment on formatMatchCount.

Create apps/desktop/src/components/terminal/TerminalSearch.css. Position: absolute, top: 8px, right: 8px, z-index: 20 (above split controls at z-index 10). Use var(--bg-secondary) background, 1px solid var(--border-color) border, border-radius: 6px, box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3), display: flex, align-items: center, gap: 4px, padding: 4px 8px. Input styles: background var(--bg-tertiary), color var(--text-primary), border 1px solid var(--border-color), border-radius 4px, font-size 13px, width 180px, outline none, padding 2px 6px. .terminal-search-input--no-match modifier: border-color #ef4444. Match count span: color var(--text-secondary), font-size 11px, white-space nowrap, min-width 80px, text-align center. Buttons: 22px square, same pattern as .terminal-split-btn in Terminal.css.

In Terminal.tsx, import TerminalSearch from './TerminalSearch'. Import './TerminalSearch.css' at the top of Terminal.tsx. In the JSX return, inside .terminal-container and after the .terminal-content div, render: {isSearchOpen && searchAddonRef.current && <TerminalSearch searchAddon={searchAddonRef.current} onClose={() => { setIsSearchOpen(false); xtermRef.current?.focus(); }} onQueryChange={(q) => { searchQueryRef.current = q; }} />}.

Create apps/desktop/src/components/terminal/__tests__/terminalSearch.test.ts. Import formatMatchCount and isNoMatch from '../TerminalSearch'. Write at least 8 test cases covering: formatMatchCount returns '' for empty query, 'No results' for non-empty query with found=false, 'Match found' for found=true; isNoMatch returns false for empty query (regardless of found), false when found=true, true when query is non-empty and found=false, false for whitespace-only query (trim check).

In KeyboardShortcuts.tsx, add { keys: ['Ctrl', 'F'], description: 'Find in terminal buffer' } as the last entry in the Terminal group's shortcuts array.
tasks: Create TerminalSearch.tsx exporting formatMatchCount and isNoMatch pure helpers at top of file, with props { searchAddon, onClose, onQueryChange }, 150ms debounce via useRef + setTimeout, auto-focus via useRef + useEffect, Escape/Enter/Shift+Enter key handlers on input

Implement Previous/Next buttons calling findPrevious/findNext, Aa toggle for caseSensitive, X button calling onClose, and .terminal-search-input--no-match class applied when isNoMatch(found, query) is true

Create TerminalSearch.css with position absolute top 8px right 8px z-index 20, CSS variable color references matching the existing design system, .terminal-search-input--no-match modifier, and button styles matching the .terminal-split-btn pattern

In Terminal.tsx: import TerminalSearch, import './TerminalSearch.css', and render <TerminalSearch> inside .terminal-container when isSearchOpen && searchAddonRef.current is non-null, with onClose restoring focus and onQueryChange syncing searchQueryRef
Create apps/desktop/src/components/terminal/__tests__/terminalSearch.test.ts with at least 8 test cases for formatMatchCount and isNoMatch covering empty query, whitespace, found=true, found=false
In KeyboardShortcuts.tsx Terminal group, add { keys: ['Ctrl', 'F'], description: 'Find in terminal buffer' } as the final shortcuts array entry
Run pnpm test and verify all terminalSearch tests pass alongside existing tests
Run pnpm typecheck and verify zero TypeScript errors
Run pnpm build and verify tsc -b strict compile passes
verification: pnpm test passes with apps/desktop/src/components/terminal/__tests__/terminalSearch.test.ts included — all formatMatchCount and isNoMatch cases green
pnpm typecheck passes with no errors across TerminalSearch.tsx, Terminal.tsx, and KeyboardShortcuts.tsx
pnpm build passes (tsc -b strict) with no new errors; TerminalSearch is rendered conditionally inside .terminal-container in Terminal.tsx
TerminalSearch.css uses z-index: 20 and references only var(--bg-secondary), var(--bg-tertiary), var(--text-primary), var(--text-secondary), var(--border-color) from the existing CSS variable set
KeyboardShortcuts.tsx Terminal group shortcuts array contains the Ctrl+F entry as its final item
